title: bee-executing: worker consult protocol (D3 loop, evidence bundle, authority carve-out, Consults report section)
action: Per D1/D3/A1/A2/A4: add an Advisor Consult section to the worker protocol. Trigger: only when the dispatch prompt carries an Advisor line, and only after the FIRST serious failed verify attempt. Canonical loop: fail 1 -> consult 1 -> advised retry -> (fail) -> consult 2 (follow-up, same advisor) -> final retry -> [BLOCKED] with a Consults section (count, advisor identity, one-line ask/answer digest per consult). Max 2 consults per claim; a re-dispatched cell starts a fresh budget. Evidence bundle is mandatory: exact command, failing output, diagnosis, cited file excerpts, CONTEXT.md path — passed inline in the consult prompt or piped via stdin, never a /tmp path (critical pattern 20260708); never secrets or env values. Transport (proven, validation report in read_first): the Advisor line names the advisor and how to consult — model shape: the worker's own Agent tool, model param set, description starting exactly 'advisor-consult <cell-id>: <advisor-model>' so the dispatch log attributes it (A2); cli shape: run the given command with the bundle on stdin. A transport error (non-zero exit, rejected dispatch, hang past the External Executors timeout discipline) is NOT advice, burns at most one budget slot total, and is never retried in a storm. Advice is advice-only (A1): it never authorizes installs, gate approvals, or file scope beyond the cell; advice conflicting with a locked D-ID -> [BLOCKED] citing both. Amend the line-68 rule to: after the first serious failure consult if an Advisor line exists; two serious failures with no consult budget remaining -> [BLOCKED] (unchanged path when no advisor). State explicitly that the headless no-questions rule (lines 91-93) is unchanged — a consult is not a question to the parent or user. When capping, characterize the pre-change rule by quoting the old line 68 as red_failure_evidence.

truths: a worker with no Advisor line in its dispatch behaves exactly as today (two failures -> [BLOCKED])
the consult loop is stated as fail 1 -> consult 1 -> advised retry -> consult 2 -> final retry -> [BLOCKED], max 2 per claim
authority-type blocks (ambiguous cell, uncapped deps, architecture, package install, locked-decision conflict) are named as never consulting

artifacts.substantive: Advisor Consult section + amended two-attempts rule; headless rule explicitly unchanged

key_links: the section references the evidence-bundle stdin rule and the Consults report fields that bee-swarming's goal-check reads
prohibitions: no gate-time or orchestrator consult language (D1 — de967733 amended, not reversed)
no weakening of the verify-evidence cap rules

trace.outcome: Added an Advisor Consult section (D1/D3) to bee-executing SKILL.md: worker-level, on-failure-only consult loop (fail 1 -> consult 1 -> advised retry -> consult 2 -> final retry -> [BLOCKED] with a Consults section), max 2 per claim, mandatory stdin/inline evidence bundle, proven transport (Agent-tool model-shaped w/ advisor-consult <cell-id>: <advisor> description prefix, or cli-shaped stdin), transport-error != advice, advice-only/A1 conduct, authority-block carve-out, and explicit headless-rule-unchanged (A4) statement. Amended the old unconditional two-attempts line into a no-Advisor-line (unchanged) vs Advisor-line-present branch. No gate-time/orchestrator language added.
